subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV/ECF, World Bank, ADB, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem) + Nepali official (Nepal Rastra Bank, NSO) + Nepali independent English press (Kathmandu Post, Nepali Times, Online Khabar).
A young federal democracy marked by chronic government churn and recurring 2024-25 instability and protests; the rupee is pegged to the Indian rupee, so monetary policy is effectively imported.

Standout drags: chronic political instability, severe Himalayan climate/seismic exposure, a weak narrow economy, and landlocked India-dependence; a relatively free press and hydropower upside offset. Source-lean: MODERATE-HIGH Anglophone.

economic.notes: A remittance-cushioned but narrow economy with policy imported via the Indian-rupee peg.

fiscal_state.notes: Moderate debt and a remittance-cushioned external position, but low ~3-4% growth on an IMF program. (IMF 2026 Art IV; NRB)

monetary_independence.notes: The rupee is pegged to the Indian rupee, so policy is effectively imported - very low autonomy. (NRB; IMF)

inflation.notes: Notably low and contained, India-linked. (NRB; IMF)

banking_stability.notes: Mixed - rising NPLs and excess liquidity with weak credit demand; post-stress fragility. (IMF 2026)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A huge structural trade deficit and a narrow base (agriculture, tourism), remittance-dependent, with hydropower exports to India a real long-term upside. (NRB; World Bank)

institutional.notes: A weak, corruption-dogged rule of law and capacity, with a relatively free press the bright spot.

rule_of_law.notes: Weak - low TI standing, slow politicised courts, and entrenched corruption. (TI CPI 2025; WJP)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Weak and politicised, strained by an incomplete federal transition and chronic capital-spending under-execution. (World Bank; IMF)

press_freedom.notes: Relatively free and vibrant (RSF ~87th, the region's best), with logged violations. (RSF 2026)

political_social.notes: Chronic instability and deep social cleavages, with generally free liberties.

political_stability.notes: Chronic churn - a revolving door of coalition governments since the 2008 republic - with recurring 2024-25 instability and protests; a young federal democracy that remains unstable. (Carnegie; The Diplomat)

civil_liberties.notes: Generally free formal freedoms, with a 2025 social-media-crackdown blemish since reversed. (Freedom House)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Limited - a labor-exporter (remittances ~25% of GDP), not a relocation destination. (NRB; World Bank)

social_cohesion.notes: Caste/Dalit, ethnic, and Madhesi-plains-versus-hills cleavages with federal tensions. (Carnegie; ConstitutionNet)

demographics.notes: A young population but with heavy youth out-migration draining the workforce. (UNFPA; NRB)

geopolitical.notes: A small state between India and China, India-dependent and landlocked.

alliance_reliability.notes: Squeezed between India and China, non-aligned and India-dependent. (ORF)

conflict_involvement.notes: No active conflict post-civil-war, with landlocked India-dependence the key vulnerability. (general)

physical_practical.notes: Severe Himalayan climate and seismic exposure, weak healthcare and infrastructure, with low crime a strength.

climate.notes: Glacier retreat, GLOF risk, monsoon flood/landslide, water-cycle disruption, low adaptive capacity. (IPCC AR6 cryosphere; WB CCDR)

healthcare.notes: Weak - low spend, a severe specialist/medic shortage and emigration, and uneven rural access. (Kathmandu Post; World Bank)

infrastructure.notes: Poor - chronic project delays and weak roads, landlocked, with power improving via hydropower. (World Bank; ADB)

crime_safety.notes: Relatively low violent crime - a genuine strength. (general)

disaster_insurance.notes: Very low penetration against extreme seismic/flood/GLOF exposure. (inferred)

summary: A young federal democracy whose chronic political churn - a revolving door of coalition governments since the 2008 republic, with recurring 2024-25 instability and protests - sits alongside a weak, narrow, remittance-dependent economy whose monetary policy is effectively imported through an Indian-rupee peg. The dominant physical drag is severe Himalayan exposure: glacial-melt floods, catastrophic earthquake risk, monsoon landslides, and the capital's extreme air pollution, against very thin disaster insurance. A relatively free press and substantial hydropower-export potential to India are the genuine offsets, leaving a balanced outcome distribution.
